title: Q1 Quiz 4 Draft: Lighting, Landscapes, Foliage, and Mini-Worlds

questions.text: Which light type emits light in all directions from one point?
choices: Point light
Spotlight
Rectangle light
Landscape light
questions.explanation: A point light acts like a bulb and emits light outward in all directions.

questions.text: Which light type is most like sunlight coming from far away?
choices: Directional light
Point light
Trigger volume
Foliage light
questions.explanation: Directional lights are used for sun-like light from an extremely far distance.

questions.text: What is the main purpose of the Smooth tool in Landscape mode?
choices: To soften harsh terrain bumps or sharp artifacts.
To delete all foliage from the map.
To turn every light into a skylight.
To export the project to Google Drive.
questions.correctAnswer: To soften harsh terrain bumps or sharp artifacts.
questions.explanation: Smooth reduces harsh changes in the landscape heightmap.

questions.text: Why should foliage density be adjusted instead of always left high?
choices: Too much foliage can hide the path, hurt readability, or affect performance.
Density only changes the color of the sky.
High density automatically creates Blueprints.
Low density deletes the landscape.
questions.correctAnswer: Too much foliage can hide the path, hurt readability, or affect performance.
questions.explanation: The transcript shows density being reduced repeatedly so trees and foliage do not overwhelm the scene.

questions.text: What does a Blueprint preview help students understand in the mini-world lesson?
choices: How a scene could become interactive or playable.
How to replace Google Drive submissions.
How to make every mesh static.
How to remove all lighting from a level.
questions.correctAnswer: How a scene could become interactive or playable.
questions.explanation: The Blueprint preview connects the environment to simple player interaction without requiring a full game system.

questions.text: Identify the term: a light that shines in one direction inside a cone shape.
questions.explanation: Spotlights use cone angles to control the beam and falloff.

questions.text: Identify the term: the outdoor ground surface that can be sculpted into hills, valleys, and platforms.
questions.explanation: The Landscape system is used to create and edit terrain.

questions.text: Identify the term: the route a player is encouraged to follow through a space.
questions.explanation: A player path helps make a mini-world readable and playable.

questions.text: Identify the term: a visual scripting asset used to create behavior or interaction in Unreal.
questions.explanation: Blueprints use nodes and visual scripting to create behavior without writing traditional code.

questions.text: A student lights a scene with three lights, but the viewer does not know where to look. What should they adjust?
questions.correctAnswer: They should adjust lighting direction, intensity, color, shadow, or placement to create a clear focal point.
questions.explanation: Lighting should guide attention, not just make the whole scene equally bright.

questions.text: A landscape has sharp spikes and rough artifacts after sculpting. What tool or workflow should the student use?
questions.correctAnswer: They should use Smooth to soften harsh shapes and adjust brush size or strength for more controlled sculpting.
questions.explanation: The transcript shows Smooth as the fix for harsh landscapes after aggressive sculpting.

questions.text: Trees painted with Foliage mode are leaning sideways on a slope. What setting should the student check?
questions.correctAnswer: They should check Align to Normal and turn it off if trees should grow straight up.
questions.explanation: The transcript specifically notes unchecking Align to Normal so trees do not tilt with the landscape slope.

questions.text: Put this landscape workflow in order: create landscape, sculpt broad forms, flatten a usable area, smooth harsh edges, capture evidence.
questions.correctAnswer: 1. Create the landscape. 2. Sculpt broad forms. 3. Flatten a usable area. 4. Smooth harsh edges. 5. Capture evidence screenshots.
questions.explanation: Landscape work should move from broad terrain creation into refinement and evidence capture.

questions.text: Put this mini-world workflow in order: create terrain, add main path/focal point, paint foliage, adjust density/scale, play or camera test, submit evidence.
questions.correctAnswer: 1. Create terrain. 2. Add the main path or focal point. 3. Paint foliage. 4. Adjust density and scale. 5. Play or camera test. 6. Submit evidence.
questions.explanation: The mini-world process should keep readability and player experience ahead of random decoration.

questions.text: In 2-3 sentences, explain how lighting, terrain, and foliage work together to make a mini-world feel intentional.
questions.correctAnswer: A strong answer explains that lighting creates mood and focus, terrain creates readable forms or paths, and foliage/props add believable detail without hiding the design. It should mention evidence such as screenshots, path readability, or focal point.
questions.explanation: This checks synthesis across scene lighting, landscape shaping, and mini-world finishing.
